summary: Relinked melee/ft/fighter.c as matching by changing its configure.py object status from NonMatching to Matching. To make the relink stable, the PR adjusted MSL sqrtf in src/MSL/math.h to use inline double literals 0.5 and 3.0 instead of static const double locals, which shifted local sdata2 symbol numbering in config/GALE01/symbols.txt while preserving the same addresses and data sizes.
what_changed: Changed Object(NonMatching, "melee/ft/fighter.c") to Object(Matching, "melee/ft/fighter.c") in the ft Fighters library list in configure.py.
Removed static const double _half = .5 and static const double _three = 3.0 from the extern inline sqrtf implementation in src/MSL/math.h.
Rewrote the three Newton refinement steps in sqrtf to use literal constants 0.5 and 3.0 directly.

Updated ten local .sdata2 symbol labels near 0x804D8250-0x804D8290 in config/GALE01/symbols.txt, renumbering labels such as @265 to @259 while keeping addresses, object sizes, scopes, and float/double annotations aligned.
smart_moves: The relink was minimal and targeted: only the object matching status, the math header codegen detail affecting constants, and the resulting symbol labels were changed.
Using direct floating-point literals in sqrtf avoided introducing named static local constants in the inline header, which appears to have been necessary for the fighter object to link as matching.
The symbols.txt update reflects compiler-generated local label renumbering rather than pretending the underlying addresses or data types changed.
decomp_lessons: Header inline functions can affect object-local constant pools and therefore matching status in unrelated-looking translation units that include them.
For MSL inline math helpers, replacing static const locals with immediate literals may change sdata2 emission and local symbol numbering in a way that restores matching.
When relinking a source file from NonMatching to Matching, update configure.py and any affected symbol-map local labels together so the build configuration and map metadata remain consistent.
Local @NNN symbol names are compiler/linker artifacts; if codegen changes only their numbering, preserve the same addresses, sizes, and type comments in symbols.txt.
naming_conventions: Matching build objects are declared as Object(Matching, "melee/ft/fighter.c") in configure.py.
Nonmatching build objects are declared as Object(NonMatching, "...") in configure.py.
Generated local symbols in config/GALE01/symbols.txt use @number labels for local .sdata2 constants.
Global fighter-related sdata2 symbols retain subsystem-style prefixes such as ftAnim_804D8298 and ftAction_804D82A0.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Inline constants directly in sqrtf instead of using static const double locals when matching code that expects no separately named _half/_three constants from the header.
After a header codegen change, inspect nearby .sdata2 local symbols because label numbering can shift even when addresses and object sizes remain unchanged.
Relink by flipping the object status to Matching only after resolving constant-pool differences introduced by shared headers.
follow_up_queries: Find other PRs that relink files by changing Object(NonMatching) to Object(Matching) in configure.py.
Search for MSL math.h sqrtf changes affecting sdata2 constants or __frsqrte codegen.
Look for PRs where local @NNN symbols in config/GALE01/symbols.txt were renumbered after header changes.
Check whether ftanim.c, ftaction.c, or ftparts.c later became matching after similar header or constant-pool fixes.
